items.check: The output is a finished web-sourced public research infographic, not notes, a text-only summary, a generic placeholder, or a source screenshot.
items.scoring_hint: Placeholder content should score low even if visually polished.

items.check: The image attempts all required display elements explicitly requested in task.md.
Critical required facts are accurate, grounded, and visible in the final image.
items.scoring_hint: Critical omissions or unsupported substitutions should heavily reduce this item.

items.check: Major required context is visible and grounded.
items.scoring_hint: Accept concise paraphrase when meaning is preserved.

items.check: Uses at least three public sources, with at least two formal paper/PDF-style sources, and records source labels/URLs or URL cues.
items.scoring_hint: Do not give high grounded credit for one paper, Wikipedia/blog/news-only sourcing, or unlabeled sources.

items.check: Provides exactly three insights that synthesize across sources rather than summarizing a single source.
items.scoring_hint: Credit requires grounded synthesis; unrelated facts copied from separate abstracts should receive limited credit.

items.check: Any numeric claims are directly tied to cited sources; unsupported percentages, dates, sample sizes, or effect sizes are absent.
items.scoring_hint: Full credit when no unsupported numeric claims appear; deduct for numbers that cannot be traced to a listed source.

items.check: Avoids prohibited sources, unsupported details, old scope, invented numbers, and other overclaims.
items.scoring_hint: Negative-only: award full credit when prohibited details are absent. Deduct only for explicit inclusion.

items.check: Optional details are not required; if included they must be supported by document or source text.
items.scoring_hint: Omission of optional details earns full credit.

items.check: Selects, compresses, and prioritizes long-document or multi-source evidence into a clear public visual message.
items.scoring_hint: Structuring credit requires mostly correct grounded facts; a tidy generic layout should score low.

items.check: Uses a clear visual hierarchy that makes title/topic and three evidence/insight cards primary.
items.scoring_hint: Reward correct information hierarchy, not mere neatness.

items.check: The image is readable, polished, visually coherent, and appropriate for the requested public visual.
items.scoring_hint: Visual quality should not compensate for missing grounded facts.

items.check: Core text is legible, non-overlapping, and sufficiently contrasted.

score_caps.reason: The output is not a usable public-facing image for the requested task.
Multiple critical facts are missing or wrong.
Multiple forbidden or unsupported details are included as public facts.
Most core image text is unreadable or visually incoherent.
